faqs.question: Are yellow jackets dangerous to my family in Kew Gardens?
faqs.answer: Yes, yellow jackets are aggressive and can sting multiple times, especially when defending their nest. Unlike honeybees, they don't lose their stinger and can attack repeatedly. For individuals with allergies, stings can cause severe reactions requiring immediate medical attention. Professional removal is the safest option.

faqs.question: When is yellow jacket season in Kew Gardens, Queens?
faqs.answer: Yellow jacket activity peaks in Kew Gardens from late July through October. Colonies grow largest in late summer and early fall, making them more aggressive as they protect their nests. This is when most homeowners discover nests and require professional removal services.
